originalTitle: Kalshi says it caught Trump’s teleprompter operator insider trading
summary: Kalshi users betting on what President Donald Trump would say during his speeches were reportedly up against tough competition: the president's teleprompter operator. ABC News reports that federal investigators believe Gabriel Perez - Trump's teleprompter operator since 2016 - used inside information to make bets on Kalshi, a major prediction market platform that allows users […]
content: Kalshi users betting on what President Donald Trump would say during his speeches were reportedly up against tough competition: the president’s teleprompter operator. ABC News reports that federal investigators believe Gabriel Perez — Trump’s teleprompter operator since 2016 — used inside information to make bets on Kalshi, a major prediction market platform that allows users to bet on just about everything. One category that Kalshi offers is “mentions” markets, in which users try to predict what an individual will say during high-profile events. Perez is accused of betting on what Trump would say during more than a dozen events, including his February State of the Union address, a Medal of Honor ceremony, and remarks at the World Economic Forum, according to ABC News. In total, Perez reportedly won more than $100,000 on Kalshi. Trump is scheduled to make another speech Thursday night, reportedly focusing on election integrity; Kalshi has multiple markets listed where users are betting on what he will say and how long he will speak for. “Our surveillance team promptly flagged and referred these trades to the [Commodity Futures Trading Commission] after an exchange investigation,” Robert DeNault, head of enforcement at Kalshi, said in an emailed statement to The Verge. “We have charged this individual and have been assisting regulators on this matter and provided evidence we collected, as we do in any referral.”  Kalshi didn’t respond on the record to questions about other bettors who wagered in these markets and whether they’d get their money back. Zach Fulton, a spokesperson for the CFTC, which oversees prediction markets, said the agency couldn’t confirm or deny any investigation. According to ABC News, the CFTC and Perez have discussed how to settle the case, including Perez giving back his earnings and not making similar trades in the future. Federal prosecutors declined to open a criminal investigation, ABC News reports. Concerns around insider trading are a major part of the story of prediction markets, especially over the last few years as platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket have exploded in popularity. A Google employee, politicians, campaign staff, a US soldier, and disgraced former congressman George Santos are among those who’ve reportedly used inside information to make bets on prediction markets.  Last month, Kalshi implemented rules that require users to disclose employment information before they can bet on “markets with heightened insider or manipulation risk.”
chineseTitle: 卡尔希称其发现了特朗普提词器操作员的内幕交易
chineseSummary: 据报道，卡尔什用户押注唐纳德·特朗普总统在演讲中会说些什么，面临着激烈的竞争：总统的提词器操作员。美国广播公司新闻报道称，联邦调查人员认为加布里埃尔·佩雷斯（Gabriel Perez）——自 2016 年以来特朗普的提词器操作员——利用内部信息对 Kalshi 进行押注，Kalshi 是一个主要的预测市场平台，允许用户 [...]
chineseContent: 据报道，卡尔什用户押注唐纳德·特朗普总统在演讲中会说什么，面临着激烈的竞争：总统的提词器操作员。美国广播公司新闻报道称，联邦调查人员认为加布里埃尔·佩雷斯（自 2016 年起担任特朗普的提词器操作员）利用内部信息对 Kalshi 进行投注，Kalshi 是一个主要的预测市场平台，允许用户对几乎所有东西进行投注。 Kalshi 提供的一类是“提及”市场，用户试图预测一个人在备受瞩目的事件中会说什么。据美国广播公司新闻报道，佩雷斯被指控在十多项活动中押注特朗普会说些什么，包括他二月份的国情咨文演讲、荣誉勋章仪式以及在世界经济论坛上的讲话。据报道，佩雷斯在 Kalshi 上总共赢得了超过 10 万美元。特朗普定于周四晚上再次发表讲话，据报道重点关注选举公正性；卡尔希列出了多个市场，用户可以在这些市场上押注他会说什么以及他会发言多长时间。 Kalshi 执行主管 Robert DeNault 在发给 The Verge 的电子邮件声明中表示：“在进行交易所调查后，我们的监控团队立即标记了这些交易并将其提交给[商品期货交易委员会]。” “我们已指控此人，并一直在此事上协助监管机构，并提供了我们收集的证据，就像我们在任何转介中所做的那样。”  卡尔希没有公开回应有关在这些市场下注的其他投注者以及他们是否能拿回资金的问题。负责监管预测市场的美国商品期货交易委员会 (CFTC) 发言人扎克·富尔顿 (Zach Fulton) 表示，该机构无法证实或否认任何调查。据美国广播公司新闻报道，美国商品期货交易委员会和佩雷斯已经讨论了如何解决此案，
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
